task_completion.description: Create a User Custom Field named 'Employee ID' with a 'Text' format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenProject Administration -> Custom fields page. A new custom field for Users should be created with the Name 'Employee ID' and Format set to 'Text' (or short string).

task_completion.description: Configure the custom field to be visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings form for the 'Employee ID' custom field, the checkbox labeled 'Visible' must be checked.

task_completion.description: Configure the custom field to be editable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings form for the 'Employee ID' custom field, the checkbox labeled 'Editable' must be checked.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the user administration page for Alice Johnson.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenProject Users administration page. The agent should be viewing or editing the profile of the user 'Alice Johnson' (login: alice.johnson).

task_completion.description: Assign the value 'EMP-9901' to Alice Johnson's 'Employee ID' field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the user profile form or settings for Alice Johnson. The newly created 'Employee ID' field should contain the exact value 'EMP-9901', followed by evidence of saving (e.g., clicking save, a success notification, or the value persisting on the profile view).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser to interact with OpenProject.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the OpenProject web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, API scripts, or rails console to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the inputs were entered into the actual OpenProject application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The custom field creation and user profile editing must clearly occur within the OpenProject web interface, not in a text editor, notes app, or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the browser developer tools were not used to fake the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should not show the browser's Inspect Element / Developer Tools being used to alter the DOM to artificially display the custom field or the 'EMP-9901' value.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Field Name: 'Employee ID'. Target Field Type: User Custom Field. Target Field Format: Text (string). Target User: 'Alice Johnson' (login: alice.johnson). Expected Value: 'EMP-9901'. The custom field must be configured as both visible and editable in the OpenProject administration settings.
